dimensions.note: Docs-only slice — no code path touched. The newly documented ssoSession arg names the shared SSO session (used only to phrase the remediation message); no token or secret material is documented. README example ids remain <account-id>/<vendor-account-id> placeholders.
No logging code changed. The documented `aws sso login` remediation matches the shared ssoRemediation wording emitted by preflightSsoGate.

Behavior unchanged. The README now documents the `network` kind (checked first, transient DNS/socket) and the `service` field the audit already emits — verified against this extension's own ScanErrorSchema (aws_iam_role_audit.ts: service z.string().default(""), kind mirroring the shared enum) and the _lib scan_error/aws_credentials sources. Per-account/per-role failures still degrade to scan_error rows and never abort the sweep.

No test surface changed. The full 135-test suite (including the colocated manifest/model.version/upgrades-tail metadata test) passes after the version bump, confirming the network kind and service values the README now documents are the ones emitted.
Read-only fan-out audit (iam:Get*/List*, cfn:DescribeStackResources, GetCallerIdentity) unchanged and re-runnable; a docs edit cannot affect idempotency.

The published README contract now matches shipped code: scan_error kind is network|auth_expired|access_denied|other and the service field lists iam/sts/sso/"" (the exact service literals at this extension's throw sites: profile_suffix_check "", credentials sts, get_role iam, preflight_sso sso). No SDK pins or request shapes changed.
No clients, handles, or files opened by a documentation change.

This IS the change: the README drifted behind the shared scan_error + SSO adoption and is corrected to document the `network` kind, the `service` scan_error field, and the `ssoSession` global argument. Manifest and model.version aligned at 2026.07.19.0. Wording mirrors the up-to-date sibling @jentz/aws-rds-reservations README. No real identifiers introduced.

MECHANICAL: the README's scan_error field list was diffed against this extension's ScanErrorSchema (profile, accountId, roleName, service, phase, kind, message, scannedAt — note it keys by roleName not region), confirming service z.string().default("") is documented as reads-default-"" and kind is a closed enum. The schema itself is unchanged strict z.object; the edit only makes the docs faithful to it.
role/scan_error keep lifetime 'infinite' and garbageCollection 10 — untouched by a docs change.
Read-only fan-out audit; no create/update/delete lifecycle to complete. Unaffected by a documentation change.

The stackLookupRegions fail-closed check, requiredProfileSuffix refusal, and opt-in SSO pre-flight are unchanged in code; the README now documents the ssoSession pre-flight (empty default skips it), matching preflightSsoGate's early return.
No key construction changed by a docs edit; the role/scan_error key formats are as before.
No read/write data-access code changed; the downstream integration-coverage contract (role/scan_error row shapes) is byte-identical. The README simply describes the service field accurately now.

Invariant holds: model.version bumped 2026.07.03.0 -> 2026.07.19.0 with a matching no-op upgrade entry appended (identity upgradeAttributes; description states docs-only, no resource schema changes). Chain tail equals model.version and manifest version, verified by the colocated metadata test in the passing suite.
